items.title: Controversial CIT CEO gives emphatic denial of EV training misconduct allegations

children.content: The CEO of the Canberra Institute of Technology has strongly denied allegations that student mechanics worked on Hertz hire cars without the company's knowledge, insisting the claims are "factually incorrect" and "offensive" to staff and students.

gallery.altText: A professional in front of CIT signage

thumbnail.altText: A professional in front of CIT signage

items.title: Accused exit trafficker fraudulently cancelled wife's visa application, police say

children.content: The man, who left his wife in India and did not return for the birth of his son, is the first person in the ACT to be charged with the crime.

image.altText: A concrete building reading "Law Courts of the Australian Capital Territory", with Australian, ACT and Aboriginal flag.

items.title: Man avoids jail after indecently abusing woman he met on Tinder

children.content: The ACT Supreme Court heard the man was concerned only with his own sexual gratification when he abused his victim.

image.altText: A Tinder screen.

items.title: Accused 'money mules' refused bail after allegedly moving millions from scams

children.content: Police say so-called "money mules" facilitate the swift movement of fraudulently obtained money.

image.altText: Cash

items.title: Man discovers Stolen Generations truth after decades believing he was adopted

children.content: Ricky Hampson spent most of his life believing he had been adopted before learning several years ago he is a survivor of the Stolen Generations. Today, he is calling for more action and truth-telling.

image.altText: A man looks serious in a shirt with the slogan "Always Was".

items.title: ACT public school system to be unified in 'generational shift'

children.content: The ACT's public education system will be significantly reformed after a review of resourcing found schools operated much like "independent small businesses".

gallery.altText: A group of primary school students sit in a classroom facing away.

thumbnail.altText: A group of primary school students sit in a classroom facing away.

articles.title: Israel kills new Hamas militant chief, along with his wife and children

children.content: An Israeli air strike in Gaza has killed new Hamas leader Mohammed Odeh, his wife and two of his children, the militant group has confirmed.

image.altText: People carry bodies wrapped in cloth through the streets of gaza

articles.title: Israel warns all residents in southern Lebanon to leave

children.content: It is the first time Israel has made such a sweeping demand since US President Donald Trump announced in April a ceasefire had been imposed in Lebanon.

image.altText: A large smoke cloud billows from behind residential buildings.

articles.title: Iran's internet is back on. But it's not what it used to be

children.content: Reports of an increase in internet traffic are filtering through, but experts say Iran has a long way to go to get back up to speed.

image.altText: A close-up shot of a man's hands typing on a keyboard in a dark room.

children.content: ABC experts are answering questions abut the Iran war as the conflict enters its fourth month with no clear end in sight. Follow live.

updates.title: It’s been three months since the conflict escalated. How has this impacted international travel?
Does Trump have an end goal anymore?

items.title: Young saviour and an answered prayer deliver Blues incredible Origin win

children.content: When the Blues needed heroes to get on top of the Maroons in State of Origin I, they turned to a debutante and the oldest player on the field.

image.altText: Ethan Strange celebrates a Blues try in State of Origin I.

items.title: Hugh Marks denies 'threat' to force ABC news director out of role

children.content: ABC managing director Hugh Marks says he did not "make threats" to terminate departing news boss Justin Stevens, as senators question his sudden departure.

image.altText: A man looking pensive, walking towards the camera wearing a suit and carrying a clipboard.

items.title: Man joins brother and late mother's reconciliation journey after discovering he is Indigenous

children.content: Iain Seymour didn't realise he was Aboriginal until he discovered he was adopted and learned that his mother played a key role in a memorial to the long-term effects of the Stolen Generations.
Warning: This story contains images of Indigenous people who have died.

items.title: ASX falls, government targets tech giants with tough scam laws

children.content: The Australian share market is trading lower while the federal government proposes tougher scam laws targeting tech giants. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: ASX

items.title: Mother whose two children died suddenly calls for genetic testing

children.content: A Perth woman who lost two children to an extremely rare medical condition calls for mandatory genetic testing for children whose cause of death is uncertain.

image.altText: A mother with blonde hair and a white dress stands in front of the ocean holding a young baby girl in a white outfit.

items.title: 'Remarkable' large funnel cloud spotted in regional Victoria

children.content: The Bureau of Meteorology says it is unusual to see large funnel clouds so far west in Victoria.

items.title: 'He can't help himself': Fugitive gangster's social media tracked by police

children.content: A wanted Sydney underworld figure may have unwittingly helped police after posting social media messages mocking his underworld adversaries from overseas.

image.altText: Anthony Pele standing next to a Lamborghini

items.title: Divers begin complex mission to rescue men found alive in flooded Laos cave

children.content: Five of the seven people, who entered the cave in central Laos more than a week ago, have been found alive. Rescuers are still searching for two.

image.altText: Group of villagers trapped inside Laos cave in the dark looking relieved to be found.

items.title: The most influential female composers in history and today

children.content: From Hildegard von Bingen to Hildur Guðnadóttir, here are some of the most influential women of the music world in history and today.

children.content: Forget about slashers and body horror — the next evolution of fright stems from an fluorescent-lit room.

children.content: How well do you know your Neighbours from your Deadloch? Take our single-frame Australian TV quiz to find out.

items.title: Stars involved in handshake drama as Rybakina exits Roland-Garros

children.content: There is no love lost between Tamara Korpatsch and Wang Xinyu after a testy match as Elena Rybakina's hopes of another grand slam title go up in smoke.

articles.title: 'My fault to hire him': Spaniard's coach quits mid-French Open via text

children.content: Alenajdro Davidovich Fokina's French Open comes to an end shortly after his coach flees to Miami after claiming he was unwell after a lunch catch-up.

items.title: Four Corners reporter Mahmood Fazal sacked by ABC

children.content: Mahmood Fazal has been sacked by the ABC following an internal investigation into an appearance on a podcast hosted by a former underworld figure, managing director Hugh Marks says.

image.altText: A man with a full black beard.

items.title: UK's social media ban could be harsher than Australia's. Here's how it compares

children.content: The UK could become the latest nation to implement a social media ban, after British Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer vowed to introduce a "game changer" policy.

image.altText: Keir Starmer in a dark shirt with his sleeves rolled up, siting while smiling next to two children with red hats.

items.title: Matthew Perry's assistant deserves to 'rot in prison', actor's friends say

children.content: Matthew Perry's live-in personal assistant, who had a central role in the Friends star's descent into ketamine addiction and injected him with the fatal dose of the drug, has been sentenced to three years and five months in prison.

image.altText: A picture of Matthew Perry at a film premiere.

items.title: 'Like sugar to a human': Farmers look to move away from nitrogen 'hit'

children.content: A war thousands of kilometres away from Australia's farms is quietly reshaping what crops are planted and what will be available at supermarkets in the future.

gallery.altText: Man stands for portrait among apple orchard

thumbnail.altText: Man stands for portrait among apple orchard

items.title: Legal action filed over federal government's land clearing decision

children.content: The federal environment minister's decision to wave through plans to bulldoze almost 3,000 hectares — an area 10 times the size of Sydney's CBD — of habitat, home to threatened species, will be challenged in the Federal Court.

image.altText: Aerial view of the Daly River next to Claravale Station where land has been cleared to make way for cotton crops.

items.title: Refinery on WA's south coast says it could replace region's diesel with biofuel

children.content: In the 1990s, Esperance was seen as a key to a major biofuels industry for Western Australia. Thirty years later, the industry has seen little change. But some say the area has untapped potential as a major fuel supplier.

image.altText: a petrol pump

items.title: Social media group calls for change while exposing inequality in sport

children.content: Not All Clubs calls out inequality in sporting organisations across the country. This weekend, the group is calling on teams to wear red armbands in memory of the women whose lives have been claimed by gender-based violence this year.

image.altText: Two AFL players stand in a huddle. Both players wear two red bands on their left arm.

items.title: Torquay wagtails increasing in numbers around the world

children.content: A Victorian artist paints hundreds of willie wagtails a week to give away locally and now they're popping up all over the world.

gallery.altText: Mike McLean paints his willie wagtails on pieces of reclaimed timber.

thumbnail.altText: Mike McLean paints his willie wagtails on pieces of reclaimed timber.

items.title: Fears for teacher who last contacted family a week ago after trip to India

children.content: The family of maths teacher Sunil Sharma say the 66-year-old disappeared nearly a week ago, while trying to sell a property in the Punjabi city of Amritsar.

image.altText: Surbhi and Sunil Sharma smile, taking a selfie together in a city.

items.title: Modelling suggests 'second wave' of algal bloom in Spencer Gulf

children.content: Modelling predicts that the algal bloom that has plagued South Australia's coastlines since March 2025 will be moving further through the Spencer Gulf in the next few months.

image.altText: A man sits at a table near the beach with a laptop.

items.title: Man charged with murder after several people attacked in regional Queensland

children.content: A 36-year-old man has been charged with murder after allegedly attacking several people south of Bundaberg.

image.altText: A police car parked on a road lined with trees.

children.content: The ACT Supreme Court heard the man was concerned only with his own sexual gratification when he abused his victim.

items.title: A pipe bursts every 73 minutes in Qld, report warns situation set to worsen

children.content: Queensland councils are calling for more state funding to update the state's pipe network, which they say has deteriorated to a high-risk condition.

items.title: Specific needs 'soar' across school system over past decade, principal says

children.content: The WA government has committed $16 million for a professional learning hub that will include mandatory inductions for special needs education assistants and disability modules for graduate teachers.

image.altText: Students are having a math class.

items.title: 'How low will they stoop?' Apology demanded over leak to media

children.content: A Tasmanian independent politician is calling for an apology after her personal health-related request via email to the government was leaked to a media outlet.
